zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Hugh Lowther III於1288年出生在英國維斯特莫藍德洛福黑 卡素時，他的父親Sir Hugh Lowther II23歲，母親Lady Margaret de Lucy19歲。他和Margaret De Lucy至少有5個兒子和1個女兒。他去世於1367年在英國英格蘭維斯特莫藍德洛福黑 卡素享年79歲。

nameMeanings.name: Lowther
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from a place in Cumbria, so called from the river on which it stands. The placename is of obscure etymology, perhaps of ancient Welsh origin (compare Lauder ), or from Old Norse lauthr ‘froth, foam’ + á ‘river’.

variants.name: Lawther
meanings: Scottish, northern Irish, and northern English: variant of   Lowther   and   Lauder  .

variants.name: Lauder
meanings: Scottish (Borders): habitational name from Lauder in Berwickshire, originally a name for the river Leader (itself from British lou- + dubro- ‘cleansing river’). Compare Lauderdale .

nameMeanings.name: Hugh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French name, Hugues, Hugo, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from hug ‘heart, mind, spirit’. It was originally a short form of various compound names containing this element. This was borne by the aristocracy of medieval France, adopted by the Normans, and introduced by them to Britain. It was consistently one of the most popular boys' names in the Middle Ages in England, but fell out of favour in the 16th century until it was revived in the 20th century. Little Hugh of Lincoln was a child supposed in the Middle Ages to have been murdered by Jews in about 1255 , a legend responsible for several outbursts of anti-Semitism at various times. The story is referred to by Chaucer in The Prioress's Tale. He is not to be confused with St Hugh of Lincoln ( 1140–1200 ), bishop of Lincoln ( 1186–1200 ), who was noted for his charity and good works, his piety, and his defence of the Church against the State. Since the later Middle Ages Hugh has also been used as an Anglicized form of the Gaelic name Aodh .

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Hugh Lowther III was born in 1288, in Lowther Castle, Westmorland, England, his father, Sir Hugh Lowther II, was 23 and his mother, Lady Margaret de Lucy, was 19. He had at least 5 sons and 1 daughter with Margaret De Lucy. He died in 1367, in Lowther Castle, Westmorland, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 79.

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Hugh Lowther III(이)가 1288년 영국 웨스트모를랜드 로터 카스틀에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Hugh Lowther II(은)는 23세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Margaret de Lucy(은)는 19세였다. 그는 Margaret De Lucy(와)과 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1367년 영국 잉글랜드 웨스트모를랜드 로터 카스틀에서 79세의 나이로 사망했다.
